verses.1: Therefore, I urge elders among you, as your fellow elder and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and one who is also a fellow partaker of the glory that is to be revealed:
verses.2: shepherd the flock of God among you, exercising oversight, not under compulsion but voluntarily, according to the will of God; and not with greed but with eagerness;
verses.3: nor yet as domineering over those assigned to your care, but by proving to be examples to the flock.
verses.4: And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
verses.5: You younger men, likewise, be subject to your elders; and all of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because GOD IS OPPOSED TO THE PROUD, BUT HE GIVES GRACE TO THE HUMBLE.
verses.6: Therefore humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God, so that He may exalt you at the proper time,
verses.7: having cast all your anxiety on Him, because He cares about you.
verses.8: Be of sober spirit, be on the alert. Your adversary, the devil, prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
verses.9: So resist him, firm in your faith, knowing that the same experiences of suffering are being accomplished by your brothers and sisters who are in the world.
verses.10: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, who called you to His eternal glory in Christ, will Himself perfect, confirm, strengthen, and establish you.
verses.11: To Him be dominion forever and ever. Amen.
verses.12: Through Silvanus, our faithful brother (for so I regard him ), I have written to you briefly, exhorting and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it!
verses.13: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings, and so does my son, Mark.
verses.14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to you all who are in Christ.
